D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Cadmium Sulphide CdS — Bandgap 2.42 eV Yellow

CdS sits at the sweet spot — its 2.42 eV bandgap absorbs everything above 512 nm (cyan-violet), leaving the yellow-orange-red half to reflect; impressionists loved it

Cadmium yellow (CdS) was patented in 1817 and became the artist standard for permanent yellow by 1840. Bandgap 2.42 eV → absorption edge at 512 nm → all wavelengths shorter (blue/violet/UV) absorbed, longer (yellow/orange/red) reflected. Monet, Renoir, Van Gogh: all the bright yellow you see in Impressionism is essentially CdS.

Particle-size slider tunes the bandgap via quantum confinement: small nano-particles widen E_g and shift colour toward greenish-yellow; bulk CdS is the canonical yellow.
